name: Risotto alla Milanese
tagline: Saffron risotto from Milan — bone marrow optional, parmesan mandatory
highlight: Saffron-gold, creamy without cream, naturally nut-free

hero.alt: Saffron-yellow risotto with parmesan and gold sheen
hero.caption: risotto milanese — saffron and parmesan

healthFacts: Saffron contains crocin — antioxidant pigment with antidepressant effects in studies.
Risotto rice (carnaroli) releases starch slowly — silkier than arborio.

steps.title: Bloom the saffron
steps.detail: Warm stock in a saucepan. Steep saffron in a ladle of warm stock 10 minutes — releases full color and aroma.
steps.tip: Saffron straight into rice is wasteful — bloom it first.

steps.title: Toast the rice
steps.detail: Sweat onion in 1 tbsp butter and oil over medium 4 minutes. Add rice; toast 2 minutes until edges turn translucent.
steps.tip: Toasting the grain seals it so it cooks creamy on the outside, al dente in.

steps.title: Wine, then stock by ladle
steps.detail: Pour in wine and stir until absorbed. Add saffron stock first, then plain stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly. ~18 minutes total.
steps.tip: Stirring releases starch — that's where the creaminess comes from.

steps.title: Mantecare
steps.detail: Off heat, beat in remaining butter and parmesan vigorously. Rest 1 minute, covered. Plate — should pool, not pile.
steps.tip: Mantecare = beat in cold butter for a glossy emulsion. The signature Italian finish.
